notes: The full ridiculous recipe: tequila, coconut rum, Crème de Menthe, a smidgin of Campari, Angostura bitters, topped with grapefruit juice. Cost £7.
Ordered at the Nag's Head.
Del orders: 'Oi John. Giss a Remi Martin with cream soda and lots of ice, and 'arf of lager.'
Del brags: 'I said get down that cellar and sort me out a bottle of Vin Ordinaire!' - thinking it means something posh.
Del orders defiantly: 'a large chivas Regal with coke!' Rodney then orders a double Southern Comfort with American dry - but Del overrides: 'That's 'alf a lager for Rodney.'

episode_title: The Long Legs of the Law
notes: Del orders: 'Giss a Tia Maria and a pineapple juice and, er, 'alf a lager for lover boy.'
Grandad recalls his mum: 'with her rum and pep in one hand, 20 Senior Service in the other. She looked like a lady.'

cocktail: Drambuie with Lime, Soda, Ice, Lemon and Cherry
notes: Del's elaborate order: 'a large Drambuie with lime, topped up with soda, lots of ice, slice of lemon and a cherry.' Barmaid gives him two halves of lager.
Del tries to impress in Spain: 'Garçon, dos Dubonnet pore favore. Danke schon.'
Del casually offers: 'Tea, coffee, Pina Colada?' - more comic offer than bar order.
Del offers Miranda: 'port and lemon, rum and coke? Or shall I surprise you?'
Del's homemade cocktail - actually made with gin because he ran out of tequila. 'That is called a Tequila Sunset. Cheers.'
Del's usual: 'Yeah, Campari and diet coke please!'
Del orders: 'a Singapore Sling and half of Strongbow.'
Del orders at the Nag's Head: 'Yeah, Grand Marnier and grapefruit.'
Del orders: 'I'll have a Malibu and tonic with some lime and 'alf of lager, please darling.'
Barmaid lists Del's recent drinks: 'Tuesday it was a Bacardi and Russian.'
Barmaid: 'last week it was Grand Marnier and Orange.'
Barmaid: 'the week before that was Dubonnet and Coke.'
One of the signature drinks. 'Del sips his Tia Maria and Lucozade. He doesn't know what it is, but he likes it.'
Grandad tried to get Del one but they'd sold out - got him a Mackeson instead.
Del warns Albert: 'You'll feel even worse if I catch you at my Courvoisier again.'

episode_title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
notes: Del orders: 'giss a Manhattan Mike, small rum for him. Spit in the rum.'

episode_title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
notes: Del orders: 'giss a banana daiquiri for us moi and Australian lager for Jumbo.' Mike: 'I only sell British lager, Del.'
June's drink of choice - she totters in with a glass of it. 'I'm on Benedictine and lemonade.'
Albert's nightcap - he accidentally orders 'a bum and rack!'
Del jokes: 'It's lucky he didn't fancy a Bucks Fizz, innit?'
The maid says she'll 'never drink Sangria again' after Del's visit.
Reen orders: 'I'll have a port and lemon. Better make it a small one.'
Del orders: 'a Malibu and cherryade for moi, and one for yourself.'
Del orders: 'a Singapore sling for me and a small rum for Elton John over there.'
Del mentions having 'a couple of pina coladas' before talking about religion.
Del orders: 'pina colada for me, please, Michael; same again for Denzil.'

cocktail: Pina Colada with Ice and Alka-Seltzer
notes: After a heavy night: 'Juan. Can I have a pina colada with ice and Alka Seltzer?'
Rodney orders: 'Can I have a large Bacardi and coke, please?'
Del orders: 'A pina colada for me Nerys, and the usual for everyone else.'
Danny spots Del's abandoned drink: 'A Castella, a Malibu reef. Are you sure he's not around?'
Pam offers Armagnac. Del: 'Yeah, that'll do if you're out of brandy.' - not realising Armagnac IS brandy.
Del orders: 'Give us a peach daiquiri and a chipolata sandwich.'
Boycie: 'I'll have a large cognac.' Del: 'A cognac, an orange juice and my usual.'

episode_title: He Ain't Heavy, He's My Uncle
notes: Del orders: 'How about a large snowball? Mike, a large snowball for Dora, please.'
Del orders: 'for Rodney and a Manhattan for moi.'

episode: If They Could See Us Now
episode_title: If They Could See Us Now
notes: Del's big celebration round: 'a Singapore Sling for me, a cognac for Boycie, vodka and lime for Marlene, a Cubre Libre for Denzil...'

episode: If They Could See Us Now
episode_title: If They Could See Us Now
notes: Part of Del's big round. Del says 'Cubre Libre' - his mangled pronunciation.

episode: If They Could See Us Now
episode_title: If They Could See Us Now
notes: Del orders: 'A rum and Black for Bobby Crush' - meaning Albert.
Del on someone blowing their money: 'Blown it all on Cuba libres and lairy suits.'
Del pours himself one at the cocktail bar while Raquel sets the table. Still his go-to after all these years.
Del's dodgy wine sold to the Nag's Head. Mike serves the remainder of the 'Romanian Rheisling.'
Mentioned in fan compilations but not found in any script.
Often mentioned in fan lists but not found in scripts.
Commonly repeated by fans but not pinned to an episode.
Fan-list item. Searched scripts - no mention of Baileys found.
Likely a reversed-memory duplicate of Blackcurrant and Pernod.
Fan-memory candidate. Del actually says 'Spitzer' (his mangled version) in Yuppy Love S6E1, but it's white wine and mineral water, not red.
Del's yuppie phase drink: 'I never liked Spitzers before, but now I'm right into 'em.' Also offers one to Trigger. Scene of the famous bar flap fall.
Del orders 'a bottle of Beaujolais Nouveau... a '79' - not realising Nouveau means this year's vintage. The girls laugh.
Very funny but speculative. Tizer appears in scripts but only as a comparison, not as a drink order.
